remember: Identify and recall the general formula for alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes.
List and describe the physical properties of alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes.
State the general types of reactions of alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes.
Recall the names of different types of hydrocarbons (saturated and unsaturated).
understand: Explain the difference between saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons.
Describe the preparation of alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes.
Discuss the properties and uses of different types of hydrocarbons.
Explain the concept of isomerism in hydrocarbons.
apply: Derive the structure of a given compound using IUPAC nomenclature.
Predict the products of different types of reactions of hydrocarbons.
Determine the type of reaction that can occur in a given compound.
Calculate the molar mass of a given hydrocarbon.
analyze: Compare and contrast the physical properties of different types of hydrocarbons.
Critically evaluate the uses of different types of hydrocarbons.
Analyze the environmental impact of the production and use of hydrocarbons.
Design a synthesis route for a given compound.

ncert_objectives: To understand the general characteristics and types of hydrocarbons.
To describe the preparation, properties, and uses of alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes.
To explain the concept of isomerism in hydrocarbons and its importance.
board_exam_objectives: To identify and name different types of hydrocarbons according to IUPAC nomenclature.
To predict the products of different types of reactions of hydrocarbons.
To calculate the molar mass of a given hydrocarbon and describe its physical properties.
To explain the environmental impact of the production and use of hydrocarbons.
